description:
You are acting as a mobile build-and-release engineer who wants to take a quick, reproducible snapshot of the exact **pip** environment that the current CI runner is using.

  Goal  
  ────  
  1. Make sure a directory called  
     /home/user/mobile_ci  
     exists (create it if it is missing).  
  2. With one single terminal command, write the full output of  
     pip freeze --all  
     to a text file called  
     /home/user/mobile_ci/ci_env_freeze.log  
     (overwriting the file if it already exists).

  Very important formatting rules for **ci_env_freeze.log** (this is what the automated grader will check):  
  • The file must be plain UTF-8 text.  
  • It must contain at least one non-blank line.  
  • Lines must be exactly the raw lines produced by the **pip freeze --all** command (do not add, remove, or re-format anything).  
  • A newline must end the final line of the file.

  You only need a single shell command to accomplish the task (for example by chaining commands with && or ;).  Do **not** print anything extra to the console other than what the command itself naturally outputs.  No manual text editing—let **pip freeze** produce the contents.

  Once the command finishes, the file /home/user/mobile_ci/ci_env_freeze.log should be present and non-empty.  The grader will inspect only the existence of the file, that it is non-empty, and that it ends with a newline, so the exact package list does not have to match any predefined template.
truth:
The automated tests will verify the following:

  1. The directory /home/user/mobile_ci exists and is a directory.  
  2. The file /home/user/mobile_ci/ci_env_freeze.log exists.  
  3. The file is larger than 0 bytes.  
  4. The very last byte of the file is the newline character (0x0A).

  The contents themselves are **not** compared to any fixed string, only the above structural requirements are enforced.
